what_it_does: Defines a standalone HTML preview card showing a soft-gold grouped-settings panel with two columns of checkbox-like menu and navigation options.
observations: Includes dsCard metadata for group, name, subtitle, and viewport.
Loads shared _card.css and adds file-local inline styles.
Uses CSS variables for typography, colors, radii, and foreground tokens.
Renders explanatory lead copy above a gold-tinted panel.
Panel is a two-column CSS grid with grouped option lists.
Checkbox visuals are built from span elements and pseudo-elements, with an off state hiding the checkmark.
Includes circular info badges beside both group headings and one underlined link-style Floorplan label.
standouts: Soft-gold wash panel is presented as the container pattern.
The visual emphasis is on grouped settings without border or shadow.
Checked and unchecked states are demonstrated in the same mock.
conventions: Standalone preview HTML with embedded component-specific CSS.
Design-token-driven styling through CSS custom properties.
Static visual mock rather than interactive form controls.
Uses small, dense grouped controls inside a rounded tinted panel.
concerns: Checkbox-like controls are non-semantic div/span markup, so the preview does not model accessible form behavior.
Lead copy says 12 px radius while the CSS uses var(--r-lg), leaving the exact radius dependent on external tokens.
The label "Show Hide Navigation option" reads ambiguously from this file alone.
notes: A compact preview specimen for the soft-gold grouped-settings panel pattern.
questions: What exact radius does var(--r-lg) resolve to?
Are these rows intended to represent real checkboxes or only static design specimens?
Should the link-styled Floorplan item indicate navigation, emphasis, or selected state?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the rendered content: it demonstrates a soft-gold, borderless, shadowless grouped-settings container.
intention_signals: It seems intended to document when and how to use the brand's soft-gold panel as a grouped settings container before using outlined card treatments.
novelty: The panel positions a color wash itself as the container affordance, with no border or shadow.
contribution: Contributes a soft-gold grouped-settings container pattern, including tokenized spacing, radius, two-column grouping, inline option rows, info badges, checked and unchecked visual states, and a link-style option treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone design-system HTML preview of a soft-gold, rounded, borderless grouped-settings panel with two columns of checkbox-like options.
intention_for_embedding: Created to communicate the intended branded section-panel treatment for grouped settings in a design handoff context.
